content.full_text:
January 14, 1991

Ms. Colleen McGee  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. McGee:

I am writing to inquire if it is necessary to report my community interest in my spouse's income since his employer is not located in or doing business in Barstow's jurisdiction. I am a designated 730 filer as Municipal Records Administrator of the City of Barstow. I am also an elected City Clerk, however, that is not a designated position. The facts are as follows:

1. My husband works for Lockheed Support Systems, Inc. 12 miles outside the City limits at Daggett Airport.

2. Lockheed Support Systems contracts with the U.S. Government to repair Army helicopters from Ft. Irwin, CA, 45 miles from the City.

3. Lockheed Support Systems or its parent company, Lockheed, owns no property in the city's jurisdiction. Personal property is owned at the airport, 12 miles from the city. And real property is owned by the parent company at Helendale, approximately 20 miles from the city.

4. The only business Lockheed does in the jurisdiction is the purchase of nominal supplies as the government contractor with government monies. No business is obtained in the jurisdiction nor is there any office in the jurisdiction.

Your written response will be appreciated.

Sincerely yours,

A. Kay Vinson  
220 E. Mt. View  
Barstow, CA 92311
